summary: Frontend is a Vite + React SPA with no detected persisted auth tokens and no current high/critical npm advisories, but deployment-blocking gaps remain in API base URL fallback behavior, URL token handling, CSRF contract clarity for cookie-auth, blob open safety, and static security header/CSP baseline.

findings.title: API base URL insecure fallback to localhost
findings.description: Frontend API base falls back to localhost when env is missing.
findings.impact: Production builds can silently call localhost on user machines, breaking auth and risking unintended local request targeting.
findings.evidence: API_BASE_URL defaults to http://localhost:8000/api/v1 and Docker ARG default mirrors it.
findings.recommended_fix: Remove fallback for non-dev usage and fail build/startup when VITE_API_BASE_URL is missing or localhost in production.
validation_steps: Build fails when VITE_API_BASE_URL is unset in production mode.
Built bundle contains only approved API origin(s).

findings.title: Cookie-auth flow lacks visible CSRF contract
findings.description: Credentialed requests are enabled without visible frontend CSRF token/header handling.
findings.impact: If backend CSRF protection is weak/missing, authenticated state-changing requests could be forged cross-site.
findings.evidence: Axios uses withCredentials: true; no CSRF request-header attachment pattern found.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement explicit CSRF token header strategy in frontend and enforce verification in backend for mutating routes.
validation_steps: Mutating requests without CSRF token are rejected.
Cross-site forged requests cannot perform authenticated mutations.

findings.title: Invitation/reset tokens handled in URL query and shown in UI
findings.description: One-time credential tokens are passed in query strings and reset token is rendered in page UI.
findings.impact: Tokens can leak via browser history, screenshots, logs, and referrer propagation.
findings.evidence: URLSearchParams usage for token/invitation and direct token display in reset page.
findings.recommended_fix: Read token once, exchange it quickly, scrub query from URL with history.replaceState, and do not render raw token in visible UI.
validation_steps: Token is removed from URL immediately after first parse.
Token is no longer shown in UI.
Replay and expiry behavior validated with backend.

findings.title: Blob file open flow lacks frontend MIME allowlist enforcement
findings.description: Blob responses are opened in new tabs without strict MIME allowlist checks.
findings.impact: Unexpected active content types can be rendered in browser context, increasing phishing/script execution risk.
findings.evidence: createObjectURL + window.open without MIME gate in preview flows.
findings.recommended_fix: Allowlist safe MIME types for inline open; force download or block disallowed/unknown types.
validation_steps: text/html and application/javascript responses are blocked.
Allowed MIME types open correctly; unknown types download safely.

findings.title: Static-hosting security header policy is incomplete
findings.description: Current static config lacks CSP and Permissions-Policy; HSTS is not defined here.
findings.impact: Missing browser hardening weakens defense against XSS/clickjacking/policy bypasses.
findings.evidence: Only X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Referrer-Policy are set in nginx config.
findings.recommended_fix: Add CSP and Permissions-Policy in static hosting path and enforce HSTS at TLS terminator/CDN.
validation_steps: Headers are present on deployed responses, not only local container.
CSP report-only baseline passes before enforce mode.

findings.title: No axios timeout and no consistent cancellation controls
findings.description: API client has no global timeout and request-heavy views lack consistent cancellation.
findings.impact: Hung requests and stale updates degrade reliability and can amplify failure during API stress.
findings.evidence: Axios instance has no timeout; hooks/effects do not systematically abort pending requests.
findings.recommended_fix: Set global timeout and standardize abort/cancellation in long-running effects and heavy data views.
validation_steps: Slow API simulation triggers timeout handling.
Route/page switch aborts obsolete requests safely.

findings.title: Raw backend error details are surfaced directly in UI notifications
findings.description: UI helper forwards backend detail/message strings directly to user-facing toasts.
findings.impact: Internal backend information can leak to end users.
findings.evidence: apiErrorMessage returns response.data.detail or response.data.message directly.
findings.recommended_fix: Allowlist/sanitize user-safe error text and keep raw backend details only in telemetry.
validation_steps: Internal server errors produce generic user-safe message.
Raw detail remains available only in secure logs/telemetry.

findings.title: Missing explicit route/global error boundaries
findings.description: Router tree lacks route-level error elements and app root lacks explicit global error boundary.
findings.impact: Unhandled runtime errors can produce blank screens or unstable error surfaces.
findings.evidence: createBrowserRouter route config has no errorElement entries.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a root error boundary plus route errorElement fallback pages with safe messaging.
validation_steps: Forced render error results in controlled fallback UI.
No stack traces are shown to end users.

findings.title: Third-party avatar rendering leaks metadata externally
findings.description: Avatar generation uses external service with user-provided seed.
findings.impact: External service receives request metadata and seed values.
findings.evidence: Avatar URL points to api.dicebear.com with seed parameter.
findings.recommended_fix: Move avatar rendering behind backend proxy/cache or internal generator; minimize seed sensitivity.
validation_steps: No sensitive identifier used in avatar seed.
Referrer policy prevents excessive leakage.

findings.title: Duplicate Vite config files increase drift risk
findings.description: Two Vite config files with overlapping concerns can diverge.
findings.impact: Security/build settings can be inconsistent across environments and tooling.
findings.evidence: Both .ts and .js Vite config files exist in repo.
findings.recommended_fix: Use one canonical Vite config (prefer TypeScript), remove duplicate generated config.
validation_steps: Dev/build runs consistently using one config file.
No drift between duplicate config artifacts.

findings.title: Frontend CI lacks explicit build/lint/test gate and uses mutable action tags
findings.description: Security workflow does not enforce frontend build/lint/typecheck/test as blocking checks and action refs use mutable major tags.
findings.impact: Broken frontend can still pass security workflow, and mutable CI dependencies increase supply-chain risk.
findings.evidence: Workflow includes audit/scans but lacks dedicated frontend quality gate and uses @v4/@v5 refs.
findings.recommended_fix: Add blocking frontend jobs (build, lint, typecheck, tests) and pin third-party actions by commit SHA.
validation_steps: Introduce intentional frontend build failure and verify CI blocks merge.
Verify all actions resolve to pinned SHAs.

findings.title: Strict CSP compatibility risk from inline styles
findings.description: Inline style props may conflict with strict style-src CSP enforcement.
findings.impact: Future strict CSP rollout may break UI elements (toasts/layout) unexpectedly.
findings.evidence: Inline style usage in toaster and notification components.
findings.recommended_fix: Move inline styles to class-based CSS or deploy nonce/hash CSP strategy with validation in report-only mode.
validation_steps: CSP report-only shows no style violations on critical flows.
Strict CSP can be enabled without functional regressions.

quick_wins: Remove localhost API fallback and enforce explicit VITE_API_BASE_URL.
Scrub invitation/reset query tokens from URL immediately after first read.
Stop rendering raw reset token in UI.
Add MIME allowlist gate before opening blob URLs.
Add axios timeout default and begin cancellation standardization.
Add CSP and Permissions-Policy headers in frontend static hosting configuration.
Add frontend build/lint/typecheck/test CI gate and pin GitHub actions by SHA.
pre_deployment_checklist: VITE_API_BASE_URL is required and points to approved non-localhost API domain for each environment.
Cookie-auth CSRF contract is implemented and verified on mutating requests.
Invitation/reset tokens are scrubbed from URL and not shown in UI.
Blob preview flow blocks unsafe MIME and safely handles unknown content.
CSP, frame, referrer, content-type, and permissions headers are active in deployed responses.
Frontend CI blocks merge on build/lint/typecheck/test failure.
Actions in security-critical workflows are pinned by commit SHA.
post_deployment_checklist: Verify deployed bundle never targets localhost API.
Validate CSRF failures and successes from real frontend domain.
Verify token query data is absent from browser history and referrer telemetry.
Run MIME abuse test for file preview endpoints.
Confirm CSP report-only violations are resolved before enforcing CSP.
Validate runtime error telemetry captures sanitized context without sensitive data leakage.
unknowns: Backend CSRF implementation details and SameSite cookie policy are not verifiable from frontend code.
Backend single-use and TTL guarantees for invitation/reset tokens require runtime verification.
Backend file-serving MIME/content enforcement requires staging/prod validation.
CDN/edge header policy and HSTS enforcement must be validated in deployed environment.
Repository branch protection enforcement must be confirmed in GitHub settings.

optimization_targets: Consolidate API client request helpers after timeout/cancellation hardening lands.
Reduce duplicate token parsing logic in auth-related pages once secure token flow is finalized.
Optimize large data table rendering after request lifecycle hardening.
hardening_targets: API base env validation and no-localhost policy
Token URL scrubbing and secure one-time token UX
Blob MIME allowlist and safe open/download behavior
CI gate hardening and immutable action pinning
tests_to_add: Unit test: API client init fails when VITE_API_BASE_URL missing in production mode.
Unit test: token query scrubbing removes token from URL after first parse.
Unit test: blob preview blocks disallowed MIME and allows PDF/image.
Integration test: error mapper redacts backend internal details for user toasts.
Integration test: route error boundary renders fallback page on thrown render error.
CI test gate: break build fixture must fail frontend workflow.
frontend_runtime_checks: Browser devtools network check for API origin correctness.
CSRF challenge checks for authenticated mutating calls.
Security header inspection from deployed CDN/edge endpoints.
CSP report-only event monitoring during staging smoke tests.
Token leak checks via URL/referrer/session replay attempts.
